evals.name: no-args-uninitialized
evals.expected_output: Brief devpace introduction, recommends /pace-help quickstart, appends 3-line navigation footer (commands/theory/next).

assertions.text: Checks for .devpace/ existence and detects uninitialized state
Outputs brief devpace introduction (1-2 sentences)
Recommends /pace-help quickstart as primary next step
Appends 3-line navigation footer with commands/theory/next
Does NOT attempt to read state.md or backlog files
Only loads help-procedures-overview.md, no other procedures

evals.name: no-args-with-active-cr
evals.expected_output: Context-aware help overview detecting active developing CR. Suggests /pace-help dev for usage details. Appends navigation footer.

assertions.text: Reads .devpace/state.md for current work state
Detects developing CR and mentions it in output
Suggests /pace-help dev for learning command usage
Appends 3-line navigation footer
Output is concise, not a full status report

evals.name: commands-default
evals.expected_output: Shows core 5 commands only (pace-init/dev/status/review/next) with one-line Chinese descriptions. Hints at /pace-help commands all for full list.

assertions.text: Globs skills/pace-*/SKILL.md to discover all skills
Reads SKILL.md description fields from discovered skills
Output lists exactly 5 core commands: pace-init, pace-dev, pace-status, pace-review, pace-next
Each command has a one-line Chinese description
Does NOT list advanced/specialized commands in default mode
Hints at /pace-help commands all for complete list
Only loads help-procedures-commands.md, no other procedures

evals.name: commands-all
evals.expected_output: Shows all commands organized in 5 tiers (core/business/advanced/specialized/system). System tier marked as auto-invoked.

assertions.text: Output has 5 tier sections: core, business, advanced, specialized, system
Core tier lists pace-init, pace-dev, pace-status, pace-review, pace-next
Business tier lists pace-biz with subcommand hints
System tier marks pace-learn and pace-pulse as auto-invoked
Does NOT list pace-help itself to avoid recursion
Navigation hint suggests /pace-help <command-name> for details

evals.name: command-detail-dev
evals.expected_output: Detailed usage guide for /pace-dev: purpose, subcommands from routing table, trigger phrases from description, related commands from NOT-for.

assertions.text: Normalizes 'dev' to 'pace-dev' and reads skills/pace-dev/SKILL.md
Outputs one-sentence purpose description
Lists subcommands extracted from SKILL.md routing table
Shows trigger phrases extracted from description keywords
Shows related commands extracted from NOT-for section
Only loads help-procedures-detail.md, no other procedures
Navigation hint at end suggests /pace-help commands

evals.name: quickstart-uninitialized
evals.expected_output: 5-step quickstart guide for uninitialized project: pace-init, pace-biz, pace-dev, pace-review, pace-status. First step highlighted.

assertions.text: Checks .devpace/ existence and detects uninitialized
Outputs 5-step numbered quickstart guide
Step 1 is /pace-init
Steps include pace-biz, pace-dev, pace-review, pace-status
Highlights first step as immediate action
Navigation hint at end for more help

evals.name: faq
evals.expected_output: Outputs FAQ list from knowledge/_guides/help-faq.md. Each entry has question, brief answer, and command reference. Footer suggests alternatives.

assertions.text: Reads knowledge/_guides/help-faq.md
Outputs FAQ entries with Q/A format
Each answer includes a command reference (e.g., /pace-dev, /pace-review)
Covers multiple categories: getting started, development, changes, releases
Footer suggests /pace-theory for concepts and free-form questions
